methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Epoch Biodesign is a Series A biotech company founded by Jacob Nathan and Douglas Hamilton that has raised $18.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies across Biotech & Health—3 public firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private counterparts at Series B and adjacent stages, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for Series A value, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $37M. Peer-median valuation across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables generates $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B; the median reflects companies valued between $600M and $3.3B at Series B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (7.4x median across the three public tickers) was not applicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure, common among private-stage biotech.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $31M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $421M. The wide spread reflects the inherent uncertainty in early-stage biotech valuations and material heterogeneity within the peer set. Key sensitivities include technology validation stage, intellectual property scope, and clinical development timeline—factors not quantified in this dataset.
